pattern: Maximizing a binary string is a front-loading greedy with one exception: every '1' should move as far left as possible, except one '1' has to stay behind to keep the very last position odd.
hints: Ignore the oddness requirement for a moment -- what arrangement of the same digits would be the largest binary number on its own?
Putting every '1' before every '0' gives the biggest possible number, but that arrangement ends in '0' whenever there is at least one zero, which breaks the odd requirement.
Set aside exactly one '1' for the final character, then pack every remaining '1' at the front followed by all the '0's in between.

edge_inputs.description: a single character that is already odd
an even mix of ones and zeros out of order
already in the optimal arrangement, nothing should move
no zeros to place in the middle at all

stress.description: random binary strings across the length range, always containing at least one '1'
